title: grants — entitlements
description: Six entitlement keys. Values seeded per Tenant by the entitlements module (from Subscription plan + BusinessType.default_config.entitlements). Enforcement lives in the observer / issuer + the policies — a 402 response indicates the tenant needs to upgrade; distinct from 403 (permission-based refusal).

entitlements.description: Base grant CRUD. Available on every plan. When false (should never happen — this is the baseline), the entire module returns 402 on every write.
Concurrent-cap on active (non-terminal) grants per tenant. Prevents runaway grant sprawl on smaller plans. Free: 25 active grants. Small: 200. Medium: 2000. Enterprise: unlimited.
entitlements.consumedOn: Grant creation (checked at check-time, not decremented via consume)

entitlements.description: CSV bulk-issue endpoint availability. Medium plan+. Below medium, `POST /api/v1/grants/bulk` returns 402 GRANT_BULK_NOT_ENTITLED.
Ability to issue grants with expires_at = NULL (never expires). Enterprise-only. Other tiers force expires_at ≤ now() + settings.grants.expiration.max_ttl_days (default 365).

Ability to issue grants with decision='deny' (an inviolable block that short-circuits every RBAC + grant check). Enterprise-only — deny is a serious control and requires the operational maturity that comes with the enterprise support tier.

Rare enterprise workflow: grant a permission the grantor does not hold, gated by a matching workflow/approvals template. Without this entitlement, the privilege-escalation guard refuses regardless of approval-flow context. Enterprise + `workflow/approvals` module co-active.

Enable cross-tenant audit-report generation on the platform-admin endpoint. Enterprise + compliance module co-active. Free / small / medium tiers cannot generate these — the endpoint returns 402 with an upsell.
